keyPoints: Validating requirements ensures they are correct, complete, and consistent.
Requirements modeling uses scenarios (like use cases) to describe how users interact with the system.
Validation techniques include reviews, prototypes, and test-case generation to uncover errors early.
commonMistakes: Confusing validation (are we building the right system?) with verification (are we building the system right?).
Creating overly complex or technical scenario models that stakeholders cannot understand.
Relying solely on one validation method instead of using a combination for a more thorough check.
